summary:
Fixed the race condition in counter increments.

Root cause:
incrementCounter was performing a non-atomic read-modify-write sequence:
1. GET current value
2. +1 in JS
3. SET new value
Under high concurrency this lost updates.

Fix:
Replaced the sequence with Redis’ atomic INCR command.

Changed file: app/counter.js
Old logic (read-modify-write) → new logic:
async function incrementCounter() {
  const client = createClient();
  const newValue = await client.incr(COUNTER_KEY); // atomic
  logger.debug(`Counter incremented -> ${newValue}`);
  return newValue;
}

Running test.sh now passes:
Expected: 100
Got:      100
PASS: Counter is correct
